task_completion.visual_evidence: The project header or sidebar clearly displays 'E-Commerce Platform' as the active project.

task_completion.description: Select 'Task' as the work package type
task_completion.visual_evidence: The new work package form is open and the type dropdown or indicator explicitly shows 'Task'.

task_completion.description: Enter the required subject for the work package
task_completion.visual_evidence: The subject input field contains the exact text: 'Implement user account dashboard with order history'.

task_completion.description: Assign the work package to Alice Johnson
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Assignee' field in the work package form shows 'Alice Johnson'.

task_completion.description: Set the status of the work package to 'In Progress'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Status' dropdown or field in the work package form shows 'In Progress'.

task_completion.description: Save and successfully create the work package
task_completion.visual_evidence: A green success notification (e.g., 'Successful creation') is visible, or the final saved work package view is displayed showing the correct subject, assignee, and status.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the application
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser window (like Chromium/Firefox) is visible with the address bar showing localhost:8080, rather than terminal commands like curl.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine OpenProject interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the standard OpenProject layout, menus, and styling, not a plain text editor or mock HTML page.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's developer tools (Inspect Element, Console) are not open in any of the screenshots.
